# manager.trainer.get_path_model { #spotforecast2_safe.manager.trainer.get_path_model }

```python
manager.trainer.get_path_model(name, iteration, model_dir=None)
```

Yield the path to a model file for a given iteration and model name.

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name      | Type                                                                                             | Description                                                                       | Default    |
|-----------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| name      | [str](`str`)                                                                                     | Model name (e.g. ``"lgbm"``, ``"xgb"``).                                          | _required_ |
| iteration | [int](`int`)                                                                                     | Iteration of the model.                                                           | _required_ |
| model_dir | [Optional](`typing.Optional`)\[[Union](`typing.Union`)\[[str](`str`), [Path](`pathlib.Path`)\]\] | Directory where models are stored. If *None*, defaults to :func:`get_cache_home`. | `None`     |

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type                   | Description                                      |
|--------|------------------------|--------------------------------------------------|
| Path   | [Path](`pathlib.Path`) | Full path where the model file should be stored. |

## Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

```python
>>> import tempfile
>>> from pathlib import Path
>>> from spotforecast2_safe.manager.trainer import get_path_model
>>> with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
...     p = get_path_model("lgbm", 3, model_dir=tmpdir)
...     p.name
'lgbm_forecaster_3.joblib'
```
